quarterly_allocation.review_notes: Q2 recap: Tudor over-performed (+18% on allocation), Rolex under-performed (-12% driven by Submariner premium compression). Rebalancing Q3 toward Tudor and Omega. Grand Seiko added as exploratory allocation — operator booked two in June with strong margin. Strong-signal mix target bumped from 55 to 60 after Q2 data validated the bias.

session_artifacts.cycle_brief_md:
# Quarterly Allocation — 2026-Q3

## Q2 Recap

| Brand | Allocation | Realized | Delta |
|---|---|---|---|
| Tudor | $35,000 | $41,300 | **+18%** |
| Omega | $28,000 | $27,400 | -2% |
| Rolex | $50,000 | $44,000 | **-12%** |
| other | $17,000 | $16,200 | -5% |

### Drivers

- **Tudor over-performance**: BB GMT steel momentum held all of Q2.
- **Rolex under-performance**: Submariner premium compressed by
  roughly 4% vs. Q1 highs; fewer clean flips.
- **Grand Seiko exploratory**: two booked in June, both closed with
  >8% net margin. Merits a dedicated allocation.

## Q3 Allocation

| Brand | Q3 Capital |
|---|---|
| Tudor | **$40,000** (+$5k from Q2) |
| Omega | $30,000 |
| Rolex | $45,000 (-$5k) |
| Grand Seiko | **$15,000** (new) |
| other | $20,000 |
| **Total** | **$150,000** |

## Inventory Mix Target

- Strong signal: **60%** (up from 55%)
- Normal signal: **30%**
- Weak signal: **10%** (floor; for one-off opportunistic buys)

## Review notes

Q2 data validated the Strong-signal bias. Bumping the target mix
from 55 to 60 reflects that. If Q3 landing is within +/- 3% of this
mix we treat it as noise; drift beyond that triggers a re-look.
